title: Build Expo Go QR scanner flow
goal: Use the phone camera through Expo Go to scan QR codes and fetch book details from Laravel.
implementation_steps: Request camera permission when scanner screen opens.
Show a permission request state before access is granted.
Show a helpful denied-permission message if access is denied.
Configure scanner to detect QR codes.
When QR is scanned, immediately pause scanning to prevent duplicate API requests.
On success, navigate to book details screen and pass returned book data or store it in route-safe state.
On failure, show a clear error and allow user to scan again.
Add a manual 'Scan Again' button after error.
Add a visual scanning frame or instruction text.

scanner_behavior.permission_denied: Show message explaining camera permission is required.
scanner_behavior.valid_scan: Fetch book details and navigate to details screen.
scanner_behavior.invalid_scan: Show error and reactivate scanner only after user taps Scan Again.
scanner_behavior.duplicate_prevention: Use an isProcessing flag to prevent repeated lookup calls.

acceptance_criteria: Expo Go prompts for camera permission.
A valid book QR opens book details.
Repeated scans do not spam backend.
User can return from details and scan another book.
Scanner works on a physical phone with LAN IP or ngrok backend URL.
manual_test_requirements: Test with an existing generated QR code from Backend storage/public qrcodes.
Test with manually generated QR containing a known books.isbn value.
Test with a random QR that does not match any book.
